disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Burning lower-back pain that is diffuse and spreads around without settling in one spot
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe the pain — what does it feel like and where exactly is it?
Is the pain in one spot or does it move around?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's a burning feeling all through my lower back — it doesn't stay in one spot, it kind of spreads around.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Even light touch to the lower back is felt as excruciating
trigger_question_examples: Is your back tender to touch?
What happens if something lightly touches or brushes your back?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Even resting a light hand on it feels unbearable — like the lightest touch is agony.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Ordinary painkillers are not helping at all, and the maximum safe daily amount of paracetamol and ibuprofen has already been taken today
trigger_question_examples: What have you taken for the pain, and how much?
Have the painkillers helped at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've already taken the most I'm allowed of Panadol and Nurofen today and it's done nothing — I'm so desperate I could just take more.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Bowel and bladder control are normal, there is no numbness around the groin/saddle area, and the legs still take weight normally
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any loss of control of your bladder or bowels?
Any numbness around your groin, buttocks or inner thighs — the area you'd sit on a saddle?
Are your legs still taking your weight normally, or any new weakness or numbness in them?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my waterworks and bowels are normal, I don't have any numbness down below, and my legs are still holding me up fine.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I don't know, I'm just focused on my back — it's all I can think about.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Feeling panicky, breathing fast, and unable to cope with the pain while alone at home
trigger_question_examples: How are you coping with all this right now?
Are you feeling anxious or panicky?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm panicking, my breathing is all fast, and I just can't cope with it anymore tonight.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: This is a flare of long-standing back pain that started after minor exertion; previous scans showed only minor findings
trigger_question_examples: Have you had this kind of back pain before?
Do you know what set it off this time?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've had this back pain for years and it flares up now and then. This time it started after I did a bit of light activity. Past scans only ever showed minor stuff.
